channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Ontario premier says he needs 'to sit down as quickly as possible' with Trump on tariffs
item.description: Ontario Premier Doug Ford said Monday that he needs "to sit down as quickly as possible" with President Trump regarding tariffs. ‘We're seeing inflation happening already, and it's unnecessary. We don't need to do this. We can have the two most prosperous countries in the world.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: Rubio suggests Ukraine won't return to pre-2014 borders
item.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio told reporters Monday that Ukraine was unlikely to win back all of the territory captured by Russia since 2014, as U.S. and Ukrainian officials are set to meet for talks in Saudi Arabia on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Ailia Zehra, The Hill

item.title: A cargo ship hits a tanker and they catch fire off England, with one crew member missing
item.description: LONDON (AP) — A cargo ship hit a tanker transporting jet fuel for the U.S. military off eastern England on Monday, setting both vessels ablaze and sending fuel pouring into the North Sea. All but one of the 37 crew of the two ships were brought safely ashore.
item.dc:creator: JILL LAWLESS, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: The US high court revives a case around Nazi-looted impressionist painting in Spanish museum
item.description: MADRID (AP) — The fate of a French impressionist painting once stolen by the Nazis from a Jewish woman is in question once again after the U.S. Supreme Court on Monday revived a case that could decide its ownership.
item.dc:creator: SUMAN NAISHADHAM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Canada's next prime minister Carney says transition will be quick after meeting with Trudeau
item.description: TORONTO (AP) — Canada’s next prime minister met with outgoing prime minister Justin Trudeau on Monday and vowed a quick transition. Mark Carney said he had a long meeting with Trudeau in which they discussed U.S.-Canada relations, national security issues and the timing of the handover in power.
item.dc:creator: ROB GILLIES, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Trump labels ex-Australian PM Malcolm Turnbull 'weak' amid spat
item.description: The president's attack on Turnbull came after comments Turnbull made to Bloomberg that Donald Trump's style is an "advantage" to China.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: Pope Francis now 'out of danger from death' as health condition continues to improve
item.description: Vatican officials say that Pope Francis's medical condition has improved and that he is currently “out of danger from death," as of Monday evening.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Court-martial convenes for Pentagon leaker already facing years behind bars
item.description: BEDFORD, Mass. (AP) — A court-martial convened on Monday for Massachusetts Air National Guard member Jack Teixeira, who was sentenced in federal court last year to 15 years in prison for leaking highly classified military documents after the most consequential national security breach in years.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL CASEY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ukraine will propose a limited ceasefire during talks with the U.S. in Saudi Arabia, officials say
item.description: JEDDAH, Saudi Arabia (AP) — A Ukrainian delegation set to meet with America’s top diplomat in Saudi Arabia about ending the 3-year war with Russia will propose a ceasefire covering the Black Sea and long-range missile strikes, as well as the release of prisoners, two senior Ukrainian officials said
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW LEE and HANNA ARHIROVA, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Trump turns screws on Zelensky heading into Jeddah talks
item.description: President Trump is inflicting mounting pain on Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky ahead of planned talks in Saudi Arabia on Tuesday, as U.S. officials look to secure concessions Trump can bring to Russian President Vladimir Putin for a peace deal. The summit of top U.S.
item.dc:creator: Colin Meyn, The Hill

item.title: More than 30 nations will participate in Paris planning talks on a security force for Ukraine
item.description: PARIS (AP) — Military officials from more than 30 nations will take part in Paris talks on the creation of an international security force for Ukraine, a French military official said Monday.
item.dc:creator: JOHN LEICESTER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Greenland votes amid Trump invitation to join US: What to know
item.description: Greenland's upcoming national election could set the stage for a later referendum on its future control, as President Trump has renewed his quest to take over the natural resources-rich arctic land.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Crisp, The Hill

item.title: Climate change, already causing problems on Earth, could soon create a mess for orbiting satellites
item.description: A new study finds that climate change is already causing all sorts of problems on Earth, but soon it will be making a mess in orbit around the planet too.
item.dc:creator: Seth Borenstein, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Ontario slaps 25% tax increase on electricity exports to US in response to Trump's trade war
item.description: Ontario’s premier, the leader of Canada’s most populous province, is now charging 25% more for electricity shipped to 1.5 million American homes and businesses in response to U.S. President Donald Trump’s tariff plan.
item.dc:creator: Rob Gillies, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Doctors declare Pope no longer in imminent danger, but remains hospitalized
item.description: Doctors said Monday that Pope Francis is no longer in imminent danger of death as a result of pneumonia but have decided to keep him hospitalized for several more days to receive treatment.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Secretary of State Rubio says purge of USAID programs complete, with 83% of agency's programs gone
item.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio is signaling that the Trump administration has finished its six-week dismantling of the six-decade-old U.S. Agency for International Development.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Knickmeyer, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: A cargo ship hits a tanker and they catch fire off England, with one crew member missing
item.description: A cargo ship carrying a toxic chemical hit a tanker carrying jet fuel for the U.S. military off England, setting both vessels on fire and sending fuel pouring into the North Sea.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Doctors declare Pope Francis no longer in imminent danger due to pneumonia, but remains hospitalized
item.description: Doctors said Pope Francis is no longer in imminent danger of death as a result of pneumonia but have decided to keep him hospitalized for several more days to receive treatment.
item.dc:creator: Nicole Winfield, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Ukrainian war veteran amputees learn adaptive skiing in Oregon
item.description: A group of Ukrainian war veteran amputees have been in Oregon learning a form of adaptive skiing for people with one leg.
item.dc:creator: Claire Rush And Jenny Kane, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Syria's worst violence in months reopens wounds of the civil war
item.description: An ambush on a Syrian security patrol by gunmen loyal to ousted leader Bashar Assad escalated into clashes that a war monitor estimates have killed more than 1,000 people over four days.
item.dc:creator: KAREEM CHEHAYEB, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Honduras holds primaries as voter frustration simmers over security and the economy
item.description: TEGUCIGALPA, Honduras (AP) — Voters in Honduras will select candidates from the three main parties Sunday to compete in November’s general election for the presidency in a country that remains deeply polarized.
item.dc:creator: MARLON GONZÁLEZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Mother of injured Hamas hostage directs plea to 'every mother in this world' to help secure son's release
item.description: Idit Ohel is fighting for the release of son Alon, who has been held hostage in Gaza since Oct. 7, 2023, and is at risk of going blind from an injury sustained in the Hamas attack.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: North Korea unveils its first nuclear-powered submarine
item.description: North Korea revealed its first nuclear-powered submarine, posing a threat to the United States and South Korea. Kim Jong Un has inspected its construction progress.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: UN draft report on children in conflict zones raises eyebrows with framing of Israelis
item.description: The U.N.'s annual report on children in conflict zones, which is set to be published in June, is already getting attention for its framing of Israeli victims and for omitting Hamas crimes.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Pope Francis continues to rest in hospital as Vatican’s Holy Year proceeds without him
item.description: Pope Francis remained in Rome's Gemelli Hospital on Saturday recovering from double pneumonia as the Vatican’s Holy Year proceeded without him.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Shooting in Canada leaves a dozen wounded, police say
item.description: A shooting Friday evening at a pub in Toronto, Canada, left a dozen people wounded, as police continue to search for the suspect, according to officials.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Pope Francis hits 3-week mark of hospitalization after giving world a sign of his frailty
item.description: ROME (AP) — Pope Francis hit the three-week mark Friday in his hospitalization for double pneumonia, in stable condition, receiving therapy but not necessarily improving, after giving the world a tangible indication of just how frail and sick he is.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD and MARIA CHENG, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Aid efforts in Gaza slow as millions of promised USAID dollars do not arrive, agency officials say
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — The Trump administration’s cuts to USAID have frozen hundreds of millions of dollars in contractual payments to aid groups, leaving them paying out of pocket to preserve a fragile ceasefire, according to officials from the U.S. humanitarian agency.
item.dc:creator: JULIA FRANKEL, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Hamas brushes off Trump's threat and says it will only free hostages in return for a lasting truce
item.description: CAIRO (AP) — Hamas on Thursday brushed off President Donald Trump's latest threat and reiterated that it will only free the remaining Israeli hostages in exchange for a lasting ceasefire in the Gaza Strip.
item.dc:creator: SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Hamas disregards Trump’s ‘last warning’ over hostage release
item.description: Hamas' response is an apparent dismissal of President Donald Trump's "last warning" issued on social media.
item.dc:creator: Tom Dempsey, NewsNation

item.title: Schools closed, public transport halted as rare cyclone approaches Australia
item.description: Tropical Cyclone Alfred is forecast to cross the Queensland state coast somewhere between the Sunshine Coast region and the city of Gold Coast to the south early Saturday.
item.dc:creator: JOHN PYE and ROD McGUIRK, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Trump issues 'last warning' to Hamas to release all remaining hostages held in Gaza
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump on Wednesday issued what he called a “last warning” to Hamas to release all remaining hostages held in Gaza, directing a sharply worded message after the White House confirmed that he had recently dispatched an envoy for unprecedented direct talks with the mi
item.dc:creator: AAMER MADHANI and SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: What Trump’s aid freeze means for Ukraine war
item.description: President Donald Trump’s freeze on U.S. aid going to Ukraine threatens to create severe problems for Kyiv in its war against Russia, leaving the country more vulnerable to aerial attacks on population centers and key infrastructure.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, WGN 9

item.title: 12 killed, 30 wounded in Pakistan military base attack launched by suicide bombers
item.description: PESHAWAR, Pakistan (AP) — Two suicide bombings breached a wall at a military base in northwestern Pakistan before other attackers stormed the compound and were repelled in violence that killed at least 12 people and wounded 30 others, according to officials and a local hospital.
item.dc:creator: RIAZ KHAN, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Ukraine selling T-shirts with Zelensky quote from Trump-Vance meeting
item.description: The Ukrainian government has launched a T-shirt fundraising effort seeking to capitalize on Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky's now-infamous Oval Office meeting on Friday with President Trump and Vice President Vance.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Pope stable, but will sleep with ventilation mask, Vatican says
item.description: Pope Francis was in stable condition Tuesday and breathing with just the help of supplemental oxygen after respiratory crises a day earlier, but will resume using a ventilation mask at night, the Vatican said.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Timber over Tinder: German forest has connected lovers for over a century
item.description: Known as “Bräutigamseiche” in German, the Bridegroom’s Oak has a famous knothole that's been used as a mailbox since 1892.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL PROBST and STEFANIE DAZIO, Associated Press, NewsNation
